core.readers.msr_reader module

MSR READER - Direct MSR Access with C Helper and TSC Conversion

Author: Deepak Panigrahy

This module provides MSR access using a privileged C helper binary. The C binary has CAP_SYS_RAWIO set, so Python can run without sudo.

Key Features: - Uses C helper binary (no sudo needed for Python) - Falls back to direct access if helper unavailable - Reads TSC frequency from hw_config.json for accurate C-state conversion - Converts C-state counters from TSC ticks to real time (seconds/microseconds) - CPU pinning for consistent measurements - Baseline loading (stable hardware properties) - Dynamic measurement methods for per‑run counters

Requirements Covered: - Req 1.21: Ring Bus Frequency - Req 1.27: C-State Transition Counters (now properly converted to time) - Req 1.47: Wake-up Latency - Req 1.9: Thermal Throttling

class core.readers.msr_reader.MSRReader(config: Dict[str, Any], use_baseline: bool = True, require_baseline: bool = False)[source]

Bases: object

High-performance MSR reader using C helper binary with TSC conversion.

BASELINE_PATH = PosixPath('/home/dpani/mydrive/a-lems/data/msr_baseline.json')
HELPER_PATH = PosixPath('/home/dpani/mydrive/a-lems/core/msr_helper/msr_read')
MSR_ADDRESSES = {'MSR_IA32_APERF': 232, 'MSR_IA32_MPERF': 231, 'MSR_IA32_PACKAGE_THERM_STATUS': 433, 'MSR_IA32_THERM_STATUS': 412, 'MSR_PKG_C10_RESIDENCY': 1586, 'MSR_PKG_C2_RESIDENCY': 1549, 'MSR_PKG_C3_RESIDENCY': 1016, 'MSR_PKG_C6_RESIDENCY': 1017, 'MSR_PKG_C7_RESIDENCY': 1018, 'MSR_PKG_C8_RESIDENCY': 1584, 'MSR_PKG_C9_RESIDENCY': 1585, 'MSR_PLATFORM_INFO': 206, 'MSR_UNCORE_PERF_STATUS': 1569}
average_cstate_counters(counters_per_cpu: Dict[int, Dict[str, Any]]) → Dict[str, Any][source]

Compute average of each C-state counter across all CPUs. Returns dict with raw averages and converted values.

calculate_actual_frequency(aperf1: int, aperf2: int, mperf1: int, mperf2: int) → float | None[source]

Calculate actual CPU frequency from APERF/MPERF deltas.

Formula: actual_freq = TSC_freq * (ΔAPERF / ΔMPERF)

SCIENTIFIC NOTES:

  • Ratio should be between 0 and 5 for valid CPU frequencies

  • Handles 64-bit counter wrap-around

  • Returns None if calculation is invalid

Parameters:

  • aperf1 – APERF values at start and end

  • aperf2 – APERF values at start and end

  • mperf1 – MPERF values at start and end

  • mperf2 – MPERF values at start and end

Returns:

Actual frequency in MHz, or None if invalid

calculate_wakeup_delta(start_counters: Dict[str, int], end_counters: Dict[str, int]) → int[source]

Calculate number of wakeups between two snapshots.

Each time the CPU wakes from deep sleep (C6/C7), the counter increments. By comparing start and end values, we can count total wakeups during the experiment.

Parameters:

  • start_counters – C-state counters from start of experiment

  • end_counters – C-state counters from end of experiment

Returns:

Estimated number of wakeups during the experiment

debug_cstate_snapshots()[source]

Debug method to check C-state snapshot integrity.

Call this when experiencing issues with C-state calculations to verify that snapshots contain expected data structures and no None values.

get_all_metrics() → Dict[str, Any][source]

Get complete MSR metrics including both baseline and dynamic measurements. Now includes properly converted C-state times.

get_baseline_dict() → Dict[str, Any][source]

Return a copy of the baseline dictionary.

get_ring_bus_frequency() → float | None[source]

Return cached ring bus frequency from baseline.

get_ring_limits(cpu: int = 0, pin: bool = True) → Dict[str, float] | None[source]

Extract min and max ring bus frequencies from MSR 0x621. Returns dict with ‘min_mhz’ and ‘max_mhz’ in MHz.

get_wakeup_latency() → float | None[source]

Return cached wake-up latency from baseline.

measure_hardware_wakeup_latency(cpu: int = 0, iterations: int | None = None) → float | None[source]

Measure wake-up latency using C-state counters (baseline only).

measure_ring_bus_frequency() → float | None[source]

Measure current ring bus frequency using best available method: 1. sysfs current_freq_khz (most accurate) 2. Average of min/max from MSR (fallback) 3. None if unavailable

pin_to_cpu(cpu: int) → bool[source]

Pin the current thread to a specific CPU core.

read_core_thermal_status(cpu: int = 0, pin: bool = True) → Dict[str, int] | None[source]

Read per-core thermal status from MSR_IA32_THERM_STATUS.

Some throttling events are per-core and not visible in package status. Combine with package status using OR logic for complete picture.

read_cstate_counters(cpu: int = 0, pin: bool = True) → Dict[str, Any][source]

Read C-state residency counters. Returns dict with both raw TSC ticks and converted times.

read_cstate_counters_all_cpus(pin: bool = True) → Dict[int, Dict[str, Any]][source]

Read C-state counters for all CPUs.

read_cstate_counters_for_wakeup(cpu: int = 0, pin: bool = True) → Dict[str, int][source]

Read C-state counters needed for wakeup calculation.

Returns:

Dictionary with C6 and C7 counter values

read_msr(**kwargs)
read_msr_all_cpus(msr_addr: int, pin: bool = True) → Dict[int, int][source]

Read an MSR from all CPUs.

read_thermal_throttle_status(cpu: int = 0, pin: bool = True) → Dict[str, int] | None[source]

Read complete thermal throttle status from MSR_IA32_PACKAGE_THERM_STATUS.

SCIENTIFIC NOTE:

Bit 0: thermal_now - Currently throttling (1) or not (0) Bit 1: thermal_log - Has throttled since last reset (sticky bit)

Returns:

  • thermal_now: Current throttling status

    • thermal_log: Historical throttling indicator (sticky)

Returns None if MSR read fails.

Return type:

Dictionary with

safe_get_counter(counters_dict: Dict | None, state: str, default: int = 0) → int[source]

Safely extract a counter value, ensuring it’s a number.

Use this method whenever accessing C-state counters to avoid NoneType errors. This provides a single point of defense against None values, missing keys, and type conversion issues.

Parameters:

  • counters_dict – Dictionary possibly containing counter values

  • state – State name (‘c2’, ‘c3’, ‘c6’, ‘c7’)

  • default – Default value if not found or invalid

Returns:

Integer counter value (always a number, never None)

set_end_snapshot(snapshot: Dict[str, Any])[source]

Store the end snapshot.

static set_performance_governor() → bool[source]
set_start_snapshot(snapshot: Dict[str, Any])[source]

Store the start snapshot.

snapshot_aperf_mperf(cpu: int = 0, pin: bool = True) → Dict[str, Any][source]

Take a snapshot of APERF/MPERF for before/after comparison.

Use at START and END of experiment to calculate actual CPU frequency during the measurement period.

Returns:

Dictionary with aperf, mperf values and timestamp

snapshot_cstate_counters(cpu: int = 0, pin: bool = True) → Dict[str, Any][source]

Snapshot raw C-state residency MSR counters.

Returns RAW counter values with timestamp. This is the SINGLE source of truth for C-state measurements. Unit conversion happens AFTER delta calculation using TSC frequency.

Parameters:

  • cpu – CPU core to read from (default 0)

  • pin – Whether to pin thread to CPU

Returns:

Dictionary with timestamp and counters (NEVER contains None values)

snapshot_thermal_state(cpu: int = 0, pin: bool = True) → Dict[str, Any] | None[source]

Take a complete thermal snapshot for before/after comparison.

Combines both package and core thermal status for accurate throttling detection.

CRITICAL SCIENTIFIC NOTES:

  • If both MSR reads fail, returns None (skip this experiment)

  • If one read fails, use defaults (0,0) for that domain

  • Throttling detection must use OR logic across domains

Use this at START and END of experiment to detect if throttling occurred DURING the experiment (end_log > start_log).

Returns:

Dictionary with package and core thermal status, or None if both fail

take_cstate_snapshot(cpu: int = 0) → Dict[str, Any][source]

Take a snapshot of C-state counters for wakeup calculation. Call this at start and end of experiment.

ticks_to_human(ticks: int) → str[source]

Convert TSC ticks to human-readable string.

ticks_to_microseconds(ticks: int) → float[source]

Convert TSC ticks to microseconds.

ticks_to_seconds(ticks: int) → float[source]

Convert TSC ticks to seconds.

unpin()[source]

Unpin (allow thread to run on any CPU).
